opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My chest is so tight, I can't get the air all the way out, and my inhaler isn't working anymore.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My breathing has been getting worse for hours and my usual inhaler isn't helping like it normally does.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about 6 hours ago, chest tightness and wheeze
symptom_narrative.character: tight chest, whistling/wheezy breathing, hard to blow the air all the way out
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: severe — I can hardly get a sentence out without stopping to breathe
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting up and leaning forward feels a little easier
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying down, talking too much, walking across the room
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feel like I have a cold coming on the last couple of days, really anxious about not getting enough air
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have asthma and get flare-ups sometimes, but this feels worse than usual
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't walk across the room without being really short of breath, having to sit up

past_medical_history.how_managed: salbutamol reliever inhaler and a preventer inhaler

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: used it 6 times in the last 3 hours, not working as well anymore

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: parent is at home and worried; home has dust and a pet, poor ventilation in the room

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: measured on a consumer pulse oximeter/wearable, single reading, may be affected by movement or poor perfusion

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: very frightened about not being able to breathe, wants immediate relief

communication_style.typical_message_style: short broken sentences, may need parent to help type/relay

access_barriers.transport: parent present and able to help with transport or meeting an ambulance
